text.title: Trunk-Based Branch by Abstraction Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Six months after the migration, the abstraction layer is still in place; one implementation exists; the code has an unused indirection.
antipattern.root-cause: Step 5 was never executed; cleanup ticket was never filed (or was filed and ignored).
antipattern.fix: File the cleanup ticket at step-1 merge time. Block step-4 completion until step-5 PR is open. Schedule cleanup as part of the migration plan, not as 'nice to have'.

antipattern.symptom: A single 1200-line PR creates the interface, wraps the old code, and implements the new code; review takes a week.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated BbA as a single refactor instead of five distinct shippable steps.
antipattern.fix: Split into separate PRs: one per step. Each PR is independently reviewable and revertable.

antipattern.symptom: Flag named 'disable_legacy' gets renamed to 'use_new' mid-rollout; off-by-one in conditionals; production confused.
antipattern.root-cause: Boolean-negative flag inverts logic when renamed during rollout.
antipattern.fix: Use positive naming: <area>.<verb>_<thing> (billing.use_stripe_v2). Never start flag names with disable_ / no_ / not_.

antipattern.symptom: Flag flipped to 100% on Friday afternoon; Monday morning the data team finds a silent corruption of decimals.
antipattern.root-cause: No dark-launch period; production validation skipped.
antipattern.fix: Always run a dark-launch < 1% for at least one full diurnal cycle. Compare new vs old output for a defined sample size; flip only after match-rate exceeds threshold.
